text.title: A11y Screen Status Tracker Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the A11y Screen Status Tracker methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Tracker has exactly one row per named screen / flow. Composite rows ('checkout' covering 5 screens) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Composite rows hide partial pass/fail mixes; per-screen rows are the audit unit.

rule.statement: Verdict per row is one of: pass / partial / fail / not-applicable. 'In progress' or free-form strings are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Closed enum makes the tracker filterable and groupable.

rule.statement: Each row names a single accountable owner (handle + role). 'Team' / 'design' / empty are forbidden.
`last_tested` is an ISO date; rows older than 90 days flip to status `stale` (separate from verdict).
rule.rationale: Stale rows mislead readers into thinking issues are addressed.

rule.statement: Any row with verdict ∈ {partial, fail} MUST link a remediation plan (issue tracker URL or doc URL). Empty remediation field on non-pass rows is forbidden.
rule.rationale: A failing audit row without a fix path is theatre.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent's context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-a11y-screen-status-tracker.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
